name: code2prompt
description: CLI that serializes a codebase into a structured prompt with token counting

developer-org.comment: Created and maintained by individual developer Mufeed VH (GitHub mufeedvh) with community contributions.

implementation-language.comment: Core is written in Rust (the code2prompt/code2prompt_core crates); a Python SDK (code2prompt-rs) ships as bindings to that Rust core, so the build stack is Rust.

status.comment: Actively developed; latest release v4.3.0 dated 2026-06-13 (checked 2026-07-09).

github-stars.comment: ~7.5k stars as shown on the repo (checked 2026-07-09).

form-factor.comment: Primarily a CLI (with an interactive TUI); also ships as a Rust core library with a Python SDK (code2prompt-rs), and an MCP server built on the core (odancona/code2prompt-mcp) is referenced in the official docs ecosystem.

standalone-usable.comment: Standalone CLI/library runnable from any toolchain; not welded into a single host agent.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool — not bundled inside a host agent/IDE.

install-mechanism.comment: cargo install code2prompt; Homebrew (brew install code2prompt, delivered as a prebuilt binary); pip install code2prompt-rs for the Python SDK; and prebuilt binary releases on GitHub.

retrieval-technique.comment: Serializes the whole repository (or a glob/gitignore-filtered slice) into one structured prompt with no query-time selection; a Handlebars template controls layout. No embeddings, graph, or query-driven retrieval.

query-interface.comment: The core CLI takes no query — it packs a filtered file set (none). The companion MCP server exposes a tool call that generates a prompt from a path plus include/exclude globs.

returns.comment: Emits a single prompt blob containing a source-tree/directory file list plus concatenated file contents (optionally with git diffs/logs), rendered via Handlebars.

structural-awareness.comment: No tree-sitter/AST/LSP use — files are filtered by glob/.gitignore and concatenated as plain text; the source tree is a directory listing, not a parsed symbol map.

chunking-strategy.comment: Whole files are included as units; no sub-file chunking.

reranking.comment: No retrieval/ranking stage — it packs, it does not rank.

index-location.comment: Stateless packer — builds no persistent index; regenerates the prompt on each run.

incremental-updates.comment: No index to update — each invocation re-reads and re-packs the repository from scratch.

vector-store.comment: No embeddings, so no vector store.

embedding-model.comment: n-a — code2prompt does not use embeddings. It counts tokens locally via tiktoken encodings (o200k/cl100k) but performs no semantic retrieval.

mcp-support.comment: An MCP server (odancona/code2prompt-mcp) built on the code2prompt-rs core is part of the official docs ecosystem; it exposes a prompt-generation tool. Not a first-party module in the main repo but the sanctioned MCP path.

agent-compatibility.comment: Via the MCP server it works with any MCP-capable client; docs/README call out Claude Desktop and Cursor configuration. The CLI output can also be pasted into any LLM chat.

ide-integrations.comment: Primarily a terminal CLI with an interactive TUI; no first-party VS Code/JetBrains extension.

language-agnostic.comment: Works on any text-based files regardless of language; filtering is by glob/.gitignore, not parser support.

supported-languages.value: any (text-based files) — no per-language parsing; selection via glob patterns and .gitignore, with special handling for formats like Jupyter notebooks

code-leaves-machine.comment: Runs fully locally; token counting uses local tiktoken encodings and no source is transmitted by code2prompt itself (the user then feeds the generated prompt to whatever LLM they choose).

local-only-option.comment: Runs entirely offline; no external API required.

self-hostable.comment: OSS you run locally — inherently self-hostable.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry documented in the CLI/library (checked 2026-07-09).

max-repo-scale.comment: The Rust core is fast, but the practical ceiling is the target LLM's context window since it packs a whole (filtered) repo into one prompt; glob filtering and token counting help manage this. Not monorepo-scale retrieval infrastructure.

pricing-model.comment: Free and open source under MIT.

maturity.comment: Past v4.x (v4.3.0), ~7.5k stars, stable and polished CLI still actively iterating; not yet at the ubiquity/scale of the largest tools.

active-maintenance.comment: v4.3.0 released 2026-06-13, well within 90 days of the research date (checked 2026-07-09).

community-size.comment: ~7.5k GitHub stars — a well-known packer, below repomix's scale (large) but a solid mid-size community.
